requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must contribute sufficient employee deferrals to capture full employer matching contribution in 403(b) plan, maximizing tax-advantaged compensation and retirement savings for non-profit and educational organization employees
criteria: Must be eligible for employer matching under 403(b) plan provisions
Contribute at least enough to receive maximum employer match (typically 3-6% of compensation)
Match formulas vary: dollar-for-dollar up to 3-4%, or 50% of deferrals up to 6%
Understand vesting schedule which may be immediate, cliff, or graded over time
Some 403(b) plans provide discretionary employer contributions beyond matching
Coordinate employee deferrals throughout year to maximize match (watch for true-up provisions)
Consider impact of bonus and supplemental compensation on match calculation
Review if employer offers both 403(b) matching and 457(b) opportunity for additional savings

403b_employer_match.immediate_return: Employer match provides immediate guaranteed return on employee contributions
403b_employer_match.tax_free_contribution: Match contributions not taxed when made; taxed as ordinary income when distributed
403b_employer_match.vesting_schedule: Match may vest immediately or over time; understand schedule before job change
403b_employer_match.combined_limits: Employee + employer contributions limited to $70,000 (2025) or 100% compensation
403b_employer_match.nonprofit_advantage: Non-profit employees may access both 403(b) and 457(b) for enhanced savings
403b_employer_match.true_up_provisions: Some plans provide year-end true-up ensuring full match despite contribution timing

restrictions: Must make employee deferrals to receive match; no contribution typically means no match
Matching formula varies by employer; review plan document to understand exact calculation
Vesting schedule may result in forfeiture if leave before fully vested; coordinate with career timing
Non-profit employers may have lower match rates than corporate sector 401(k) plans
Front-loading contributions may forfeit later match if no true-up and compensation varies
Employer match counts toward overall plan contribution limit reducing room for other contributions
Match typically only applies to elective deferrals, not to employee after-tax contributions
Job change before vesting may forfeit unvested match; understand vesting schedule before leaving
